channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Taiwan drills with U.S. rocket system, firing in China's direction
item.description: The drill on Wednesday marked the first time the U.S.-supplied HIMARS system has been fired into the Taiwan Strait.
item.content:encoded:
The drill on Wednesday marked the first time the U.S.-supplied HIMARS system has been fired into the Taiwan Strait.

(Image credit: Chiang Ying-ying)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Inflation tops 4% for the first time in 3 years on spike in gasoline prices
item.description: Inflation has surged to its highest level in more than three years since the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, triggering a surge in gasoline prices.
item.content:encoded:
Inflation has surged to its highest level in more than three years since the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, triggering a surge in gasoline prices.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: July 1 brings big student loan changes. Here's what you need to know
item.description: A popular (and generous) repayment plan ends, two new plans begin and many borrowers will see new loan limits.
item.content:encoded: A popular (and generous) repayment plan ends, two new plans begin and many borrowers will see new loan limits.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes. And, House passes ICE and Border Patrol funding
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have exchanged strikes after a helicopter was downed Monday near the Strait of Hormuz. And, House Republicans have passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through Trump's term.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. and Iran have exchanged strikes after a helicopter was downed Monday near the Strait of Hormuz. And, House Republicans have passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol through Trump's term.

(Image credit: Ryan Murphy)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: China re‑centers North Korea ties as nuclear silence reshapes balance
item.description: Xi's silence on North Korea's nuclear program came as Kim tried to convince the world of the irreversibility of his country's status as a nuclear power.
item.content:encoded:
Xi's silence on North Korea's nuclear program came as Kim tried to convince the world of the irreversibility of his country's status as a nuclear power.

(Image credit: 朝鮮通信社)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: ICE denies having a protester database. But a letter to Congress sheds more light
item.description: In a previously unpublicized letter to Congress, the newly-departed head of ICE said the agency collects data on people suspected of potentially unlawful activity, which could include protesters.
item.content:encoded:
In a previously unpublicized letter to Congress, the newly-departed head of ICE said the agency collects data on people suspected of potentially unlawful activity, which could include protesters.

(Image credit: Joseph Prezioso)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: San Francisco immigration court shuts down, striking at heart of historic advocacy
item.description: The main San Francisco court was one of the busiest in the country, hearing thousands of cases a year. It was also one of the courts most likely to grant an immigrants' asylum application.
item.content:encoded:
The main San Francisco court was one of the busiest in the country, hearing thousands of cases a year. It was also one of the courts most likely to grant an immigrants' asylum application.

(Image credit: Brian L. Frank for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: The World Cup is starting. Here's what to know and how to watch
item.description: This summer, 48 men's national teams will compete in the World Cup's biggest tournament ever. Here are a few of the basics to get you started.
item.content:encoded:
This summer, 48 men's national teams will compete in the World Cup's biggest tournament ever. Here are a few of the basics to get you started.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Bill Gates is appearing before Congress over Epstein involvement
item.description: Gates will sit for a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee about his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded:
Gates will sit for a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee about his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Apache helicopter is downed, takeaways from Tuesday's primaries in four states, House Republicans approve a bill to fund ICE for the remainder of Trump's term.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes after Apache helicopter is downed, takeaways from Tuesday's primaries in four states, House Republicans approve a bill to fund ICE for the remainder of Trump's term.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
